steps.title: Check current atlantis deployment
steps.description:
Verify your atlantis version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
helm list -n atlantis 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working atlantis installation.

steps.title: Review atlantis configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant atlantis configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl get configmap -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=atlantis
```

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support OpenTofu (epic)
steps.description:
A workaround for users that want to go down the opentofu path before support is official could do the following.

1. Set [`--tf-download=false`](https://www.runatlantis.io/docs/server-configuration.html#tf-download) to avoid downloading terraform versions
2. Use a custom container to [download a
```yaml
initConfig:
  enabled: true
  image: alpine:latest
  imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # sharedDir is set as env var INIT_SHARED_DIR
  sharedDir: /plugins
  workDir: /tmp
  sizeLimit: 250Mi
  # example of how the script can be configured to install tools/providers required by the atlantis pod
  script: |
    #!/bin/sh
    set -eoux pipefail

    # terragrunt
    TG_VERSION="0.55.10"
    TG_SHA256_SUM="1ad609399352348a41bb5ea96fdff5c7a18ac223742f60603a557a54fc8c6cff"
    TG_FILE="${INIT_SHARED_DIR}/terragrunt"
    wget https://github.com/gruntwork-io/terragrunt/releases/download/v${TG_VERSION}/te
```

steps.title: Upgrade atlantis to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade atlantis:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade atlantis atlantis/atlantis --namespace atlantis
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n atlantis
helm list -n atlantis
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl get events -n atlantis --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support OpenTofu (epic)" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
A workaround for users that want to go down the opentofu path before support is official could do the following.

1. Set [`--tf-download=false`](https://www.runatlantis.io/docs/server-configuration.html#tf-download) to avoid downloading terraform versions
2. Use a custom container to [download a opentofu release](https://github.com/opentofu/opentofu/releases) as `/usr/bin/opentofu_<version>`
3.
codeSnippets:
initConfig:
  enabled: true
  image: alpine:latest
  imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # sharedDir is set as env var INIT_SHARED_DIR
  sharedDir: /plugins
  workDir: /tmp
  sizeLimit: 250Mi
  # example of how the script can be configured to install tools/providers required by the atlantis pod
  script: |
    #!/bin/sh
    set -eoux pipefail

    # terragrunt
    TG_VERSION="0.55.10"
    TG_SHA256_SUM="1ad609399352348a41bb5ea96fdff5c7a18ac223742f60603a557a54fc8c6cff"
    TG_FILE="${INIT_SHARED_DIR}/terragrunt"
    wget https://github.com/gruntwork-io/terragrunt/releases/download/v${TG_VERSION}/terragrunt_linux_amd64 -O "${TG_FILE}"
    echo "${TG_SHA256_SUM} ${TG_FILE}" | sha256sum -c
    chmod 755 "${TG_FILE}"
    terragrunt -v

    # OpenTofu
    TF_VERSION="1.6.2"
    TF_FILE="${INIT_SHARE

repoConfig: |
  ---
  repos:
  - id: /.*/
    apply_requirements: [approved, mergeable]
    allow_custom_workflows: true
    allowed_overrides: [workflow, apply_requirements, delete_source_branch_on_merge]

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with atlantis installed or the issue environment reproducible.
